Vite Deployed to GitHub Pages but _plugin-vue_export-helper Returns 404
Recently I hit this — Vite builds and deploys to GitHub Pages fine, but the site doesn't actually work. Turns out
was the culprit. Writing it up.
and deploying to GitHub Pages, I started getting:

TypeError: error loading dynamically imported module
Failed to load module from "/assets/_plugin-vue_export-helper.cdc0426e.js".
And then a totally blank page. Here's how I got it working.
First instinct — path issue. So I tried hitting other built file URLs in the browser, and they all loaded fine. Only this one file kept 404'ing.
I searched "_plugin-vue_export-helper" and found this GitHub issue:
_plugin-vue_export-helper js file cant not be access with Github Pages(404)

as a filename hits a naming edge case in some environments (specifically GitHub Pages' Jekyll, which ignores underscore-prefixed files). Solution is to rename the file. Vite uses Rollup under the hood for bundling, so the rename is done through Rollup config.
The relevant Rollup source file is
— that's what handles output filenames. We're not going to edit
directly though; we override the behavior through Vite config instead.
language-js shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
// substr is deprecated, swap to slice
out of the underscore-prefixed form. Redeploy to GitHub Pages and the site loads normally.

Vite builds and ships to GitHub Pages fine — but once you load the page, the dynamically imported _plugin-vue_export-helper module 404s and the page goes blank. This post walks through the actual cause (Rollup's filename sanitization producing a name GitHub Pages doesn't serve) and the vite.config.js tweak that fixes it.
